program_status.line: Phases 1 through 6 are built. The engine is now at the point where the next gain comes from choosing a path, not rebuilding the stack.
program_status.paragraph: The system has already mapped mechanisms, causal bridges, progression objects, translational packets, endotypes, and a ranked decision slate. Right now the slate holds 4 candidate decision(s), while Phase 4 remains bounded with 0 actionable packet(s).
program_status.current_direction_line: The engine is currently steering toward Neuroinflammation / Microglial State Change biomarker panel because Future slates use this panel as the default operator readout set.

current_manuscript_candidate.title: Neuroinflammation / Microglial State Change biomarker panel
current_manuscript_candidate.story: Track NLRP3, IL-1beta, IL-6, TNF-alpha to test whether NLRP3 is moving the expected lane biology.

next_paper_opportunity.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel
next_paper_opportunity.story: Track OCLN, CLDN5, TJP1, Cerebral blood flow to test whether OCLN is moving the expected lane biology.

phases.role_line: Maps the recurring TBI mechanism lanes the rest of the engine builds on.
Shows which mechanisms are likely driving what happens next.
Tracks the recurring downstream damage states that matter over time.
Attaches targets, readouts, and intervention logic to the mechanism map.
Splits TBI into cohort and endotype patterns instead of one generic disease bucket.
Turns the structured engine into a ranked decision slate for the human operator.

major_findings: The lead mechanism in the live atlas is Blood-Brain Barrier Dysfunction.
The strongest supported bridge right now is BBB permeability increase -> peripheral immune infiltration.

The current lead insight is that Subacute Repetitive Inflammatory-Dominant discriminator panel is the best readout set to carry the next paper story, because this panel is where phase 5 endotype logic becomes measurable instead of purely descriptive.
current_unknowns: The top current uncertainty is Clarify Chronic Repetitive Tau / Clearance Mixed.
Phase 4 is still bounded. It has 6 bounded packet(s), 1 lane(s) with compound support, and 0 lane(s) with trial support.
The biggest paper blocker right now is this: The packet needs more direct cohort-level translation into chronic outcomes and transcriptomic stratifiers.

primary_decision.human_question: Do you want this to become the default panel for the next readout pass?
primary_decision.why_now: This panel is where Phase 5 endotype logic becomes measurable instead of purely descriptive.
what_we_know: Subacute Repetitive Inflammatory-Dominant is currently rated supported.
It is tied to 2 Phase 2 transition(s) and 1 downstream object(s).
It already maps to 1 endotype packet(s).
what_is_uncertain: The main open issue is this: The packet needs more direct cohort-level translation into chronic outcomes and transcriptomic stratifiers.
This item still carries novelty status: Tbi Emergent.
phase_path: Phase 1 mapped the live mechanism lane as Neuroinflammation Microglial State Change, Blood Brain Barrier Failure.
Phase 2 connected that lane through BBB Permeability Increase To Peripheral Immune Infiltration, Mitochondrial ROS To Inflammasome Activation.
Phase 3 says the downstream burden shows up as Microglial Chronic Activation.
Phase 4 attached the translational packet Neuroinflammation Microglial State Change, Blood Brain Barrier Failure.
Phase 5 says this matters most in Subacute Repetitive Inflammatory Dominant.
Phase 6 ranked Subacute Repetitive Inflammatory-Dominant discriminator panel now because This panel is where Phase 5 endotype logic becomes measurable instead of purely descriptive..

options.immediate_action: Refresh public enrichment and rebuild the decision surfaces around this panel.
options.future_effect: Future slates use this panel as the default operator readout set.
options.paper_effect: This strengthens the figures and methods path.

options.label: Validate against a challenger panel
options.immediate_action: Queue targeted extraction or manual enrichment to compare panels.
options.future_effect: Future slates surface competing panels until one wins.

options.label: Park this panel for now

primary_decision.recommended_reason: Subacute Repetitive Inflammatory-Dominant discriminator panel already links mechanism, translation, and endotype context, so it is the strongest default panel to carry into the paper path.
primary_decision.current_manuscript_effect: If you choose Adopt as the default panel, Subacute Repetitive Inflammatory-Dominant discriminator panel becomes part of the current manuscript path instead of staying only on the background slate.
primary_decision.future_engine_effect: Subacute Repetitive Inflammatory-Dominant discriminator panel will steer future machine runs and slates this way: Future slates use this panel as the default operator readout set.
primary_decision.statement: Use IL-6, IL-1beta, TNF-alpha, GFAP to discriminate the subacute repetitive inflammatory-dominant endotype from nearby cohorts.

source_row.title: Subacute Repetitive Inflammatory-Dominant discriminator panel
source_row.statement: Use IL-6, IL-1beta, TNF-alpha, GFAP to discriminate the subacute repetitive inflammatory-dominant endotype from nearby cohorts.
source_row.rationale: IL-6 / TNF-alpha / GFAP with repetitive exposure history plus DTI or white matter stress cues.
source_row.decision_rationale: IL-6 / TNF-alpha / GFAP with repetitive exposure history plus DTI or white matter stress cues.
source_row.why_now: This panel is where Phase 5 endotype logic becomes measurable instead of purely descriptive.
source_row.blockers: The packet needs more direct cohort-level translation into chronic outcomes and transcriptomic stratifiers.
source_row.next_test: Which repetitive cohorts still look barrier-conditioned in the subacute window, and which ones are already dominated by persistent microglial activation?
source_row.unlocks: A stronger cohort panel makes Phase 5 more than a literature description by giving it a real discriminator set.

secondary_decisions.human_question: Do you want this to become the default panel for the next readout pass?
secondary_decisions.why_now: This panel is already attached to a translational packet and can be tested against 4 endotype packet(s).
what_we_know: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure is currently rated supported.
It is tied to 1 Phase 2 transition(s) and 2 downstream object(s).
It already maps to 4 endotype packet(s).
what_is_uncertain: The main open issue is this: Barrier readouts can improve in tissue without reducing leakage or downstream inflammatory spillover.
This item still carries novelty status: Cross Disease Analog.
phase_path: Phase 1 mapped the live mechanism lane as Blood Brain Barrier Failure.
Phase 2 connected that lane through BBB Permeability Increase To Peripheral Immune Infiltration.
Phase 3 says the downstream burden shows up as Microglial Chronic Activation, Neurovascular Uncoupling.
Phase 4 attached the translational packet Blood Brain Barrier Failure.
Phase 5 says this matters most in Acute Mild Biomarker Imaging Bridge, Acute Severe Vascular Dominant, Acute Blast Vascular Inflammatory Mixed, and 1 more.
Phase 6 ranked Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel now because This panel is already attached to a translational packet and can be tested against 4 endotype packet(s)..

secondary_decisions.recommended_reason: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel already links mechanism, translation, and endotype context, so it is the strongest default panel to carry into the paper path.
secondary_decisions.current_manuscript_effect: If you choose Adopt as the default panel, Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel becomes part of the current manuscript path instead of staying only on the background slate.

secondary_decisions.statement: Track OCLN, CLDN5, TJP1, Cerebral blood flow to test whether OCLN is moving the expected lane biology.

source_row.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Failure biomarker panel
source_row.statement: Track OCLN, CLDN5, TJP1, Cerebral blood flow to test whether OCLN is moving the expected lane biology.
source_row.rationale: OCLN is the most evidence-dense tight-junction repair target in the current TBI corpus and best fits a barrier-module perturbation frame.
source_row.decision_rationale: OCLN is the most evidence-dense tight-junction repair target in the current TBI corpus and best fits a barrier-module perturbation frame.
source_row.why_now: This panel is already attached to a translational packet and can be tested against 4 endotype packet(s).
source_row.source_quality_mix: lane 68 full-text-like / 14 abstract-only; direct target mentions 6 full-text-like / 0 abstract-only
source_row.blockers: Barrier readouts can improve in tissue without reducing leakage or downstream inflammatory spillover; keep barrier-module claims tied to permeability outcomes, not junction markers alone.
source_row.next_test: Run an acute-to-subacute BBB repair packet around OCLN with Evans blue or gadolinium leakage, CLDN5/OCLN/TJP1 restoration, and a downstream inflammatory spillover panel.
source_row.unlocks: A cleaner panel lets Phase 4 readouts and Phase 5 endotype discrimination use the same operator-facing signal set.

secondary_decisions.human_question: Do you want the next cycle to spend real effort resolving this uncertainty?
secondary_decisions.why_now: This object currently shapes 3 endotype packet(s) and 2 translational packet(s).
what_we_know: Axonal Degeneration is currently rated provisional.
It already maps to 3 endotype packet(s).
what_is_uncertain: The main open issue is this: Object still needs denser direct support before it should be treated as hardened.
This item still carries novelty status: Tbi Emergent.
phase_path: Phase 1 mapped the live mechanism lane as Axonal Degeneration, Tau Proteinopathy Progression, Neuroinflammation Microglial State Change.
Phase 2 connected that lane through not specified.
Phase 3 says the downstream burden shows up as Cognitive Decline Phenotype.
Phase 4 attached the translational packet Axonal Degeneration, Tau Proteinopathy Progression.
Phase 5 says this matters most in Chronic Mild Metabolic White Matter Dominant, Chronic Repetitive Tau Clearance Mixed, Chronic Severe Axonal Network Decline.
Phase 6 ranked Harden Cognitive Decline Phenotype now because This object currently shapes 3 endotype packet(s) and 2 translational packet(s)..

options.label: Resolve this now
options.immediate_action: Trigger targeted extraction when possible, otherwise run manual enrichment to resolve it.
options.future_effect: Future slates will keep this uncertainty at the front until it is clarified.
options.paper_effect: This clears a blocker to publication if the hinge resolves cleanly.

options.label: Monitor only
options.what_it_steers: Keep it visible without spending the next cycle here.
options.future_effect: The hinge stays on the board at lower urgency.
options.paper_effect: The blocker remains open for now.

options.label: Reject this split for now
options.immediate_action: Record the rejected split and rebuild decision surfaces around a simpler model.
options.future_effect: Future slates stop privileging this split unless new evidence reopens it.
options.paper_effect: This narrows scope, but may miss nuance.

secondary_decisions.recommended_reason: Harden Cognitive Decline Phenotype is the main blocker on the current path, so resolving it now gives the cleanest gain in decision confidence.
secondary_decisions.current_manuscript_effect: If you choose Resolve this now, Harden Cognitive Decline Phenotype becomes part of the current manuscript path instead of staying only on the background slate.
secondary_decisions.future_engine_effect: Harden Cognitive Decline Phenotype will steer future machine runs and slates this way: Future slates will keep this uncertainty at the front until it is clarified.
secondary_decisions.statement: Cognitive decline is the downstream phenotype that makes the rest of the process engine clinically meaningful rather than merely mechanistic.

source_row.title: Harden Cognitive Decline Phenotype
source_row.statement: Cognitive decline is the downstream phenotype that makes the rest of the process engine clinically meaningful rather than merely mechanistic.
source_row.rationale: Object still needs denser direct support before it should be treated as hardened. || Some supporting rows are abstract-only and should be weighted cautiously. || Contradiction-bearing evidence is present and needs adjudication before stronger promotion.
source_row.decision_rationale: Object still needs denser direct support before it should be treated as hardened. || Some supporting rows are abstract-only and should be weighted cautiously. || Contradiction-bearing evidence is present and needs adjudication before stronger promotion.
source_row.why_now: This object currently shapes 3 endotype packet(s) and 2 translational packet(s).
source_row.blockers: Object still needs denser direct support before it should be treated as hardened. || Some supporting rows are abstract-only and should be weighted cautiously. || Contradiction-bearing evidence is present and needs adjudication before stronger promotion.
source_row.next_test: Which upstream objects or transitions are most consistently associated with chronic cognitive decline in the current corpus?
source_row.unlocks: A stronger object packet improves both biomarker ranking and endotype interpretation.
source_row.weakness_reason: Object still needs denser direct support before it should be treated as hardened. || Some supporting rows are abstract-only and should be weighted cautiously. || Contradiction-bearing evidence is present and needs adjudication before stronger promotion.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 1 · Process Lanes
phase_timeline.summary: The engine reduced the TBI literature into six recurring process lanes so the project stopped treating every paper as a one-off mechanism note.
phase_timeline.detail: Today’s decisions only make sense because the lane map already tells us which biology is actually in play.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 2 · Causal Transitions
phase_timeline.summary: The engine converted lane-level mechanisms into explicit causal bridges, so the system can say what likely drives what next.
phase_timeline.detail: Current hinge and leverage decisions are built on these bridges.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 3 · Progression Objects
phase_timeline.summary: The engine grouped recurring downstream damage states into progression objects such as chronic network dysfunction and neurovascular uncoupling.
phase_timeline.detail: The current decision slate uses these objects to explain why a choice matters downstream.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 4 · Translational Logic
phase_timeline.summary: The engine attached translational packets to each lane so targets, readouts, and intervention logic sit on top of the mechanism map.
phase_timeline.detail: Biomarker panels and leverage-point choices come directly from these packets.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 5 · Cohort Stratification
phase_timeline.summary: The engine split TBI into cohort and endotype patterns so mild, repetitive, blast, severe, acute, and chronic states stop collapsing into one disease bucket.
phase_timeline.detail: The current ambiguity around tau versus clearance is an endotype problem, not just a mechanism problem.

phase_timeline.title: Phase 6 · Hypothesis Rankings
phase_timeline.summary: The engine ranked the strongest bridges, weakest hinges, biomarker panels, leverage points, and next tasks into a weekly decision slate.
phase_timeline.detail: The portal now uses this slate as the human decision queue.

current_direction.label: Neuroinflammation / Microglial State Change biomarker panel
current_direction.reason: Future slates use this panel as the default operator readout set.
